zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Briwere於1190年7月3日出生在英國戴文斯托克時，她的父親William de Briwere II44歲，母親Beatrice de Vaux40歲。她於大約1215年與William de Percy在英國諾圖波藍德阿恩維克結婚。他們至少是3個女兒的父母。她去世於1233年6月12日在英國素瑞艾黑享年42歲，埋葬在英國素瑞艾黑。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Briwere于1190年7月3日出生在英国戴文斯托克时，她的父亲William de Briwere II44岁，母亲Beatrice de Vaux40岁。她于大约1215年与William de Percy在英国諾圖波藍德阿恩維克结婚。他们至少是3个女儿的父母。她去世于1233年6月12日在英国素瑞艾黑享年42岁，埋葬在英国素瑞艾黑。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Briwere
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Pierre, Adrien, Aurele, Benedicte, Benoit, Colette, Fernand, Gaston, Marcel, Micheline.

French (Brière): topographic name from a regional variant of Old French bruiere ‘heather’ (see Bruyere ), or a habitational name from La Brière, the name of several places mainly in the northern part of France, from the same word.
History: Jean Brière from Clarbec in Calvados, France, married Jeanne Grandin in Quebec City, QC, in 1671.

variants.name: Bruyere
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Fernand, Jacques, Marcel.

French (Bruyère): from Old French bruiere ‘heather’, hence a topographic name for someone who lived in a place where heather grew, or a habitational name from one of the places in France (for example in Calvados) named with this word. Compare Labruyere .
Altered form (Bruyère) of French Bruguier: habitational name from (Le) Bruguier, the name of several places in the southeastern part of France, a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Briwere was born on 3 July 1190, in Stoke, Devon, England, her father, William de Briwere II, was 44 and her mother, Beatrice de Vaux, was 40. She married William de Percy about 1215, in Alnwick, Northumberland, England. They were the parents of at least 3 daughters. She died on 12 June 1233, in Esher, Surrey, England, at the age of 42, and was buried in Esher, Surrey, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Briwere est née le 3 juillet 1190, à Stoke, Devon, Angleterre, son père, William de Briwere II, avait 44 ans et sa mère, Beatrice de Vaux, avait 40 ans. Elle a épousé William de Percy environ 1215, à Alnwick, Northumberland, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 12 juin 1233, à Esher, Surrey, Angleterre, à l’âge de 42 ans, et a été enterrée à Esher, Surrey, Angleterre.

Sie hat um 1215 in Alnwick, Northumberland, England William de Percy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 12. ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Briwereが1190年7月3日にイギリスデボンストクで生まれた時，父William de Briwere IIは44歳，母Beatrice de Vauxは40歳であった。約1215年にイギリスノースウンバーランドアルンウィックでWilliam de Percyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1233年6月12日にイギリススリーエッシャーで42歳で死去し，イギリススリーエッシャーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Briwere(이)가 1190년 7월 3일 영국 데폰 스토크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Briwere II(은)는 44세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Beatrice de Vaux(은)는 40세였다. 그녀는 대략 1215년 영국 노르스벌랜드 알윅에서 William de Percy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1233년 6월 12일 영국 수리 셔에서 42세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수리 셔에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Briwere nasceu em 3 de julho de 1190, em Stoke, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, William de Briwere II, tinha 44 anos e sua mãe, Beatrice de Vaux, tinha 40 anos. Ela casou-se com William de Percy aproximadamente 1215, em Alnwick, Northumberland, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 12 de junho de 1233, em Esher, Surrey, Inglaterra, com 42 anos, e foi sepultada em Esher, Surrey, Inglaterra.

ro.Stoke Damerel, Devon, England: Stoke Damerel, Devon, Anglia, United Kingdom

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Briwere родилась 3 июля 1190 г. в местности сток, девон, Англия, её отцу по имени William de Briwere II было 44 года, а матери, которую звали Beatrice de Vaux, 40. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William de Percy около 1215 г. в местности алнвикк, нортумбирланд, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 дочери. Она умерла 12 июня 1233 г. в местности эшир, сурри, Англия в возрасте 42 лет и была похоронена в местности эшир, сурри, Англия.
